title: Getting started with boldR
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("circadia-bio/boldR")

title: Getting started with boldR
code: library(boldR)  fprep <- read_fmriprep(   derivatives_dir = "data/derivatives/fmriprep",   subject         = "01",   task            = "rest",   space           = "MNI152NLin2009cAsym" ) fprep

title: Getting started with boldR
code: bold <- prepare_bold(   fmriprep    = fprep,   tr          = 2,      # seconds; or NULL to read from NIfTI header   drop_volumes = 4      # discard first 4 dummy scans ) bold

title: Getting started with boldR
text: boldR ships several built-atlases accepts custom NIfTI label image via boldR_atlas schema.
code: # See available built-ins list_atlases()  # Load Schaefer 100-parcel atlas atlas <- load_atlas("schaefer100_7n")  # Extract mean timeseries per ROI parcel <- parcellate(bold, atlas) dim(parcel$timeseries)  # timepoints × 100

previous_headings: Basic workflow > 3. Load an atlas and parcellate
title: Getting started with boldR
code: custom <- load_atlas(   atlas  = "my_parcellation.nii.gz",   labels = paste0("Region_", 1:80),   space  = "MNI152NLin2009cAsym" ) parcel_custom <- parcellate(bold, custom)

title: Getting started with boldR
code: # Functional connectivity matrix (Fisher z) fc <- compute_fc(parcel) dim(fc$z)  # 100 × 100  # ROI summary statistics roi_stats <- compute_roi_metrics(parcel) roi_stats  # Voxelwise temporal SNR tsnr <- compute_tsnr(bold) tsnr$median_tsnr

title: Getting started with boldR
code: bold_export <- export_bold(parcel, fc = fc) bold_export  # In your syncR pipeline: # syncR::sync( #   zeitR    = actigraphy_metrics, #   slumbR   = sleep_diary, #   tallieR  = questionnaires, #   bold     = bold_export # )

what: boldR — fMRI BOLD signal analysis for R
title: boldR — fMRI BOLD signal analysis for R
text: fmriprep gets clean data. boldR gets useful data. ⚠️ boldR early development formally tested. API may change without notice, estimation results yet validated reference implementation, package undergone peer review. Use caution verify outputs independently using research context. boldR R package analysing Blood-Oxygen-Level-Dependent (BOLD) fMRI data context sleep circadian research. picks exactly fMRIPrep leaves — working directly preprocessed BIDS derivatives — transforms analysis-ready outputs. boldR part Circadia Lab R ecosystem Northumbria University.

title: boldR — fMRI BOLD signal analysis for R
text: fMRIPrep input standard — reads preprocessed BIDS derivatives directly; custom preprocessing required. Atlas-agnostic parcellation — built-support common atlases (Schaefer, AAL); custom atlases accepted via simple boldR_atlas schema. Voxelwise metrics — temporal SNR, voxelwise GLM contrasts. ROI-level outputs — timeseries extraction, functional connectivity matrices, summary metrics per ROI. syncR-ready export — parcellated BOLD data flows directly syncR::sync() alongside actigraphy, PSG, questionnaire data.

title: boldR — fMRI BOLD signal analysis for R
code: # Install from GitHub (development version) # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("circadia-bio/boldR")

title: boldR — fMRI BOLD signal analysis for R
text: boldR designed used alongside:
code: fmriprep → boldR ──────────────────→ syncR                  └─→ (parcellated)

what: boldR: fMRI BOLD Signal Analysis for Circadian and Sleep Research — boldR-package
title: boldR: fMRI BOLD Signal Analysis for Circadian and Sleep Research — boldR-package
text: boldR analyses Blood-Oxygen-Level-Dependent (BOLD) fMRI data context sleep circadian research. picks exactly fMRIPrep leaves — working directly preprocessed BIDS derivatives — transforms analysis-ready outputs: atlas-agnostic parcellation, ROI-level timeseries, functional connectivity matrices, voxelwise quality metrics. fmriprep gets clean data. boldR gets useful data.

title: boldR: fMRI BOLD Signal Analysis for Circadian and Sleep Research — boldR-package
code: fmriprep → read_fmriprep() → prepare_bold()                                   ↓                             load_atlas() + parcellate()                                   ↓                     compute_fc() / compute_roi_metrics()                                   ↓                             export_bold() → syncR

title: boldR: fMRI BOLD Signal Analysis for Circadian and Sleep Research — boldR-package
text: boldR part Circadia Lab R ecosystem Northumbria University. See https://circadia-lab.uk full package suite.

title: boldR: fMRI BOLD Signal Analysis for Circadian and Sleep Research — boldR-package

what: Compute functional connectivity from parcellated BOLD timeseries — compute_fc
title: Compute functional connectivity from parcellated BOLD timeseries — compute_fc
text: Estimates ROI--ROI functional connectivity matrix parcellated BOLD timeseries using Pearson correlation (default) partial correlation. Returns full symmetric matrix tidy long-format data frame.

title: Compute functional connectivity from parcellated BOLD timeseries — compute_fc
code: compute_fc(parcellated, method = c("pearson", "partial"), fisher_z = TRUE)

title: Compute functional connectivity from parcellated BOLD timeseries — compute_fc
text: parcellated boldr_parcellated object parcellate(). method Character. Connectivity metric. One : "pearson" (Default) Pearson product-moment correlation. "partial" Partial correlation (requires corpcor Suggests). fisher_z Logical. Whether apply Fisher r--z transformation returning values. Default TRUE. Diagonal set NA.

title: Compute functional connectivity from parcellated BOLD timeseries — compute_fc
text: list class boldr_fc components: matrix Numeric matrix (n_rois × n_rois). Symmetric FC matrix NA diagonal. long Data frame (long format). Columns: roi_i, roi_j, roi_name_i, roi_name_j, fc. method Character. Connectivity method used. fisher_z Logical. Whether Fisher z applied. atlas_name Character. Atlas identifier.

title: Compute functional connectivity from parcellated BOLD timeseries — compute_fc
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ parc <- parcellate(cleaned, atlas) fc   <- compute_fc(parc) dim(fc$matrix) head(fc$long) } # }

what: Compute summary metrics per ROI — compute_roi_metrics
title: Compute summary metrics per ROI — compute_roi_metrics
text: Returns tibble per-ROI summary statistics parcellated timeseries: mean signal, standard deviation, temporal SNR.

title: Compute summary metrics per ROI — compute_roi_metrics
code: compute_roi_metrics(parcellated)

title: Compute summary metrics per ROI — compute_roi_metrics
text: parcellated boldR_parcellated object parcellate().

title: Compute summary metrics per ROI — compute_roi_metrics
text: tibble columns roi, mean, sd, tsnr, n_voxels.

title: Compute summary metrics per ROI — compute_roi_metrics
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ parcel  <- parcellate(bold, atlas) metrics <- compute_roi_metrics(parcel) metrics } # }

title: Compute voxelwise temporal SNR — compute_tsnr
text: Calculates temporal signal--noise ratio (tSNR) every -mask voxel cleaned BOLD image. tSNR defined ratio mean signal temporal standard deviation across timeseries. standard quality metric fMRI acquisitions preprocessing pipelines. Calculates temporal signal--noise ratio (tSNR) voxel BOLD image: mean signal divided standard deviation across timepoints. standard quality metric assessing fMRI data quality preprocessing.

title: Compute voxelwise temporal SNR — compute_tsnr
code: compute_tsnr(bold)  compute_tsnr(bold)

title: Compute voxelwise temporal SNR — compute_tsnr
text: bold boldR_bold object prepare_bold().

title: Compute voxelwise temporal SNR — compute_tsnr
text: list class boldr_tsnr components: tsnr 3D numeric array (x × y × z input). Contains tSNR values -mask voxels NA elsewhere. mean_tsnr Numeric. Mean tSNR across -mask voxels. median_tsnr Numeric. Median tSNR across -mask voxels. n_voxels Integer. Number -mask voxels. list class boldR_tsnr components: tsnr Numeric array. tSNR map x/y/z dimensions BOLD. mean_tsnr Numeric. Mean tSNR across -mask voxels. median_tsnr Numeric. Median tSNR across -mask voxels. bold input boldR_bold object.

title: Compute voxelwise temporal SNR — compute_tsnr
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ sub      <- read_fmriprep("data/derivatives/fmriprep", "01", task = "rest") cleaned  <- prepare_bold(sub) tsnr_out <- compute_tsnr(cleaned) tsnr_out$mean_tsnr } # } if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ fprep <- read_fmriprep("data/derivatives/fmriprep", subject = "01") bold  <- prepare_bold(fprep, tr = 2, drop_volumes = 4) tsnr  <- compute_tsnr(bold) tsnr$mean_tsnr } # }

what: Export parcellated BOLD data for syncR — export_bold
title: Export parcellated BOLD data for syncR — export_bold
text: Packages output parcellate() boldR_export object suitable ingestion syncR::sync(). export includes ROI timeseries matrix, functional connectivity matrix (computed), ROI metrics, participant metadata.

title: Export parcellated BOLD data for syncR — export_bold
code: export_bold(parcellated, fc = NULL, roi_metrics = NULL, participant_id = NULL)

title: Export parcellated BOLD data for syncR — export_bold
text: parcellated boldR_parcellated object parcellate(). fc boldR_fc object compute_fc(), NULL (default). roi_metrics tibble compute_roi_metrics(), NULL (default). NULL, metrics computed automatically. participant_id Character NULL. Override subject label use syncR. NULL, taken boldR_bold object.

title: Export parcellated BOLD data for syncR — export_bold
text: list class boldR_export ready syncR::sync().

title: Export parcellated BOLD data for syncR — export_bold
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ parcel <- parcellate(bold, atlas) fc     <- compute_fc(parcel) exp    <- export_bold(parcel, fc = fc)  # Pass to syncR syncR::sync(..., bold = exp) } # }

title: List available built-in atlases — list_atlases
text: Returns data frame describing atlases bundled boldR. Custom atlases can loaded via load_atlas().

title: List available built-in atlases — list_atlases
code: list_atlases()

title: List available built-in atlases — list_atlases
text: tibble columns name, n_rois, space, description.

title: List available built-in atlases — list_atlases
code: list_atlases() #> # A tibble: 4 × 4 #>   name           n_rois space               description                          #>   <chr>           <dbl> <chr>               <chr>                                #> 1 schaefer100_7n    100 MNI152NLin2009cAsym Schaefer 2018, 100 parcels, 7 netwo… #> 2 schaefer200_7n    200 MNI152NLin2009cAsym Schaefer 2018, 200 parcels, 7 netwo… #> 3 aal116            116 MNI152NLin6Asym     AAL 116 regions (Tzourio-Mazoyer 20… #> 4 destrieux148      148 MNI152NLin2009cAsym Destrieux 2010, 148 cortical parcels

title: Load a parcellation atlas — load_atlas
text: Loads boldR_atlas object either bundled atlas library custom NIfTI label map. boldR_atlas schema extension point user-defined parcellations.

title: Load a parcellation atlas — load_atlas
code: load_atlas(atlas, labels = NULL, space = NULL)

title: Load a parcellation atlas — load_atlas
text: atlas Character. Name built-atlas (see list_atlases()) path custom NIfTI label image. labels Character vector NULL. ROI labels, one per integer value atlas image (excluding 0). NULL atlas built-, labels loaded automatically. Required custom NIfTI inputs. space Character NULL. Template space atlas. Inferred built-ins; specified custom inputs.

title: Load a parcellation atlas — load_atlas
text: list class boldR_atlas components: name Character. Atlas name file path. nifti_path Character. Path atlas NIfTI label image. labels Character vector. ROI label integer index. indices Integer vector. Non-zero integer values atlas. n_rois Integer. Number ROIs. space Character. Template space.

title: Load a parcellation atlas — load_atlas
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ atlas <- load_atlas("schaefer100_7n") atlas  # Custom atlas atlas <- load_atlas(   atlas  = "my_parcellation.nii.gz",   labels = paste0("ROI_", 1:80),   space  = "MNI152NLin2009cAsym" ) } # }

title: boldR colour palette — palette_bold
text: named character vector eight colours used throughout boldR package pkgdown site.

title: boldR colour palette — palette_bold
code: palette_bold

title: boldR colour palette — palette_bold
text: named character vector length 8.

what: Extract ROI-level timeseries from a BOLD image — parcellate
title: Extract ROI-level timeseries from a BOLD image — parcellate
text: Applies parcellation atlas preprocessed BOLD image, returning mean timeseries per ROI. Works boldR_atlas object – built-custom – long atlas template space BOLD.

title: Extract ROI-level timeseries from a BOLD image — parcellate
code: parcellate(bold, atlas, summary_fn = mean, min_voxels = 10L)

title: Extract ROI-level timeseries from a BOLD image — parcellate
text: bold boldR_bold object prepare_bold(). atlas boldR_atlas object load_atlas(). summary_fn Function. Applied across voxels within ROI timepoint. Default mean. Common alternatives: median. min_voxels Integer. Minimum number -mask voxels ROI must contain included output. ROIs threshold returned NA timeseries warning. Default 10L.

title: Extract ROI-level timeseries from a BOLD image — parcellate
text: list class boldR_parcellated components: timeseries Numeric matrix, timepoints x ROIs. Column names ROI labels atlas. roi_voxel_counts Integer vector. Number -mask voxels per ROI. atlas boldR_atlas object used. bold boldR_bold object used. n_rois Integer. Number ROIs output. n_timepoints Integer. Number timepoints.

title: Extract ROI-level timeseries from a BOLD image — parcellate
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ fprep  <- read_fmriprep("data/derivatives/fmriprep", subject = "01") bold   <- prepare_bold(fprep, tr = 2, drop_volumes = 4) atlas  <- load_atlas("schaefer100_7n") parcel <- parcellate(bold, atlas) dim(parcel$timeseries)  # timepoints x 100 } # }

what: Validate and prepare a fMRIPrep object for analysis — prepare_bold
title: Validate and prepare a fMRIPrep object for analysis — prepare_bold
text: Checks file existence, reads NIfTI headers confirm dimensionality TR, returns annotated boldR_bold object ready downstream analysis functions.

title: Validate and prepare a fMRIPrep object for analysis — prepare_bold
code: prepare_bold(fmriprep, tr = NULL, drop_volumes = 0L)

title: Validate and prepare a fMRIPrep object for analysis — prepare_bold
text: fmriprep boldR_fmriprep object read_fmriprep(). tr Numeric NULL. Repetition time seconds. NULL (default), extracted BOLD NIfTI header. drop_volumes Integer. Number initial volumes discard (dummy scans). Default 0.

title: Validate and prepare a fMRIPrep object for analysis — prepare_bold
text: list class boldR_bold components: fmriprep input boldR_fmriprep object. dims Integer vector. BOLD array dimensions [x, y, z, t]. tr Numeric. Repetition time seconds. n_voxels Integer. Total -mask voxels. n_timepoints Integer. Number volumes dummy-scan removal. drop_volumes Integer. Volumes dropped.

title: Validate and prepare a fMRIPrep object for analysis — prepare_bold
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ fprep <- read_fmriprep("data/derivatives/fmriprep", subject = "01") bold  <- prepare_bold(fprep, tr = 2, drop_volumes = 4) bold } # }

what: Read fMRIPrep BIDS derivatives for a participant — read_fmriprep
title: Read fMRIPrep BIDS derivatives for a participant — read_fmriprep
text: Reads preprocessed fMRI outputs fMRIPrep BIDS derivatives directory single participant session, returning structured boldR_fmriprep object containing paths BOLD, T1w, T2w, segmentation files.

title: Read fMRIPrep BIDS derivatives for a participant — read_fmriprep
code: read_fmriprep(   derivatives_dir,   subject,   session = NULL,   task = NULL,   space = "MNI152NLin2009cAsym",   resolution = NULL )

title: Read fMRIPrep BIDS derivatives for a participant — read_fmriprep
text: derivatives_dir Character. Path fMRIPrep derivatives root (e.g. "sub-01/func/"... top-level derivatives/fmriprep/). subject Character. Subject label without sub- prefix (e.g. "01"). session Character NULL. Session label without ses- prefix. NULL (default), assumes single-session dataset. task Character NULL. Task label (e.g. "rest"). NULL, first task found used. space Character. Template space BOLD mask files. Default "MNI152NLin2009cAsym". resolution Character NULL. Resolution label (e.g. "2"). NULL, resolution filtered.

title: Read fMRIPrep BIDS derivatives for a participant — read_fmriprep
text: list class boldR_fmriprep components: bold Character. Path BOLD NIfTI file. bold_mask Character. Path brain mask NIfTI file. t1w Character. Path T1w NIfTI file template space. t2w Character NA. Path T2w NIfTI file available. dseg Character. Path discrete segmentation NIfTI file. confounds Character. Path confounds TSV file. subject Character. Subject label. session Character NA. Session label. task Character. Task label. space Character. Template space. derivatives_dir Character. Resolved path derivatives root.

title: Read fMRIPrep BIDS derivatives for a participant — read_fmriprep
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ fprep <- read_fmriprep(   derivatives_dir = "data/derivatives/fmriprep",   subject         = "01",   task            = "rest" ) fprep } # }

what: Register a custom brain atlas — register_atlas
title: Register a custom brain atlas — register_atlas
text: Validates registers NIfTI label map optional metadata table boldR_atlas object. schema intentionally minimal: parcellation provides 3D integer label image label--name mapping can registered passed parcellate(). Bundled atlas helpers (Schaefer, AAL, Glasser) use interface.

title: Register a custom brain atlas — register_atlas
code: register_atlas(   labels_img,   metadata,   name = "custom",   space = "MNI152NLin2009cAsym" )

title: Register a custom brain atlas — register_atlas
text: labels_img niftiImage (3D integer) file path NIfTI label map. Voxels containing 0 treated background. metadata data frame minimum two columns: label Integer. integer value labels_img. name Character. Human-readable ROI name. Additional columns (hemisphere, network, x/y/z centroid, colour, etc.) stored carried output. name Character. short identifier atlas (e.g. "Schaefer200", "AAL3", "custom"). space Character. standard space atlas defined . Default "MNI152NLin2009cAsym". Must match space used read_fmriprep() / parcellate().

title: Register a custom brain atlas — register_atlas
text: list class boldR_atlas components: labels_img niftiImage. 3D integer label map. metadata Data frame. ROI metadata. name Character. Atlas identifier. space Character. Standard space. n_rois Integer. Number unique non-zero labels.

title: Register a custom brain atlas — register_atlas
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ atlas <- register_atlas(   labels_img = "atlases/Schaefer2018_200Parcels_7Networks_MNI.nii.gz",   metadata   = read.csv("atlases/Schaefer2018_200Parcels_labels.csv"),   name       = "Schaefer200",   space      = "MNI152NLin2009cAsym" ) atlas } # }

title: boldR 0.1.0 (2026-06)
text: Package skeleton: read_fmriprep(), prepare_bold(), load_atlas(), list_atlases(), parcellate(), compute_tsnr(), compute_fc(), compute_roi_metrics(), export_bold(). Atlas-agnostic design: extensible boldR_atlas S3 class schema accepts bundled atlases (Schaefer-100, AAL-116) custom NIfTI label maps conforming boldR_atlas structure. fMRIPrep BIDS derivatives canonical input standard. pkgdown site Bootstrap 5 Circadia Lab branding.
